title: A former Thiel fellow’s startup just launched a drone it says can replace police helicopters
summary: Brinc launched its newest 911 response drone this week, as the company seeks to expand its prominence in the public safety industry.
author: Lucas Ropek

title: The BB-777 is the ultimate in boombox nostalgia
summary: Bumpboxx is fully embracing nostalgia with its latest boombox, the BB-777, which is modeled very closely on the legendary Sharp GF-777. A real deal GF-777 will set you back over $2,000 for one in working order. Plus, that vintage unit lacks modern amenities like Bluetooth or a rechargeable battery. Heck, it doesn't even have a […]
author: Terrence O’Brien

title: Amazon just bought a startup making kid-size humanoid robots
summary: The acquisition of Fauna Robotics is the second robotics startup Amazon has purchased this month.
author: Kirsten Korosec

title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
summary: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.
author: Marina Temkin

title: New Mexico just handed Meta its first courtroom defeat over child safety, and the rest of the country is watching
summary: The dollar amount isn’t as important as the fact that this is the first jury verdict of its kind against Meta over harm to young people.
author: Connie Loizos

title: Lululemon bets Epoch Biodesign can eat its shorts, literally
summary: Startup Epoch Biodesign uses enzymes to break plastic waste down into monomers, the chemical building blocks that can be used to make new plastic.
author: Tim De Chant

title: OpenAI’s Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it’s shutting down
summary: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.
author: Amanda Silberling

title: Accel, Prosus pick six ‘off-the-map’ startups for inaugural India cohort
summary: The six startups were selected from over 2,000 applications and will receive $500,000 to $2 million each.
author: Jagmeet Singh

title: Instagram and Facebook are about to be filled with affiliate content
summary: Instagram and Facebook content will soon have shopping links baked into posts, essentially cutting out third-party "link in bio"-style tools. Meta announced Tuesday that it's adding commerce features on the two platforms, though the functionality will be slightly different for each. On Facebook, content creators will be able to link their affiliate accounts they have […]
author: Mia Sato

title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
summary: A "major artificial intelligence company" reportedly offered a Kentucky family $26 million to build a data center on their farm.
author: Graham Starr

title: Meta misled users about its products’ safety, jury decides
summary: Meta willfully violated New Mexico law by misleading users about the safety of its products and engaging in an unconscionable trade practice, a jury found. The company will face a $375 million penalty for the violations, awarding the maximum penalty of $5,000 per violation for 37,500 violations across two counts. The jury decided against Meta […]
author: Lauren Feiner

title: NASA wants to put a $20 billion base on the Moon
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has big plans for the future of the agency, including the construction of a $20 billion lunar base that he said will establish an "enduring presence" on the Moon. Isaacman announced the news during NASA's Ignition event on Tuesday, where he also described goals to launch a nuclear-powered spacecraft to Mars, […]
author: Emma Roth

title: What is ICE actually doing at the airport?
summary: I arrived at John F. Kennedy International Airport nearly five hours before my domestic flight. This is not my way - usually I roll up to the airport 30 minutes before boarding - but not even I have enough hubris to think that my good luck is more powerful than a partial government shutdown. Congress […]
author: Gaby Del Valle

title: OpenAI just gave up on Sora and its billion-dollar Disney deal
summary: On Tuesday afternoon, OpenAI announced "We're saying goodbye to Sora," the video generation tool that it launched at the end of 2024, and centered in a massive licensing deal with Disney only a few months ago. The Wall Street Journal reported the move earlier, saying that OpenAI boss Sam Altman had informed staff that both […]
author: Richard Lawler

title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
summary: Anthropic’s new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.
author: Rebecca Bellan

title: Spotify tests new tool to stop AI slop from being attributed to real artists
summary: The idea behind the new tool is to give artists more control over which tracks are associated with their name on Spotify.
author: Aisha Malik

title: Arm’s first CPU ever will plug into Meta’s AI data centers later this year
summary: After decades of only licensing its chip designs for others to use, UK-based Arm revealed the first chip it's producing on its own, and the first customer. Dubbed the Arm AGI CPU, it's another chip designed for inference, or running the cloud processing for AI tools like AI agents that can continue to spawn more […]
author: Richard Lawler

title: Apple is testing a standalone app for its overhauled Siri
summary: Apple's efforts to rebuild its Apple Intelligence AI platform will make its debut at its Worldwide Developers Conference on June 8th. A new version of Siri that transforms the voice assistant into a "systemwide AI agent with deep integration across applications" will be announced at WWDC 2026, according to a new report from Bloomberg's Mark […]
author: Andrew Liszewski

title: The man who coined Metaverse now says Meta’s glasses are creepy
summary: Neal Stephenson didn't invent the virtual reality headset. But Meta certainly knows his name - in 1992, his seminal cyberpunk novel Snow Crash coined the phrase "Metaverse" to describe a virtual reality world experienced through VR goggles. It inspired many key VR developers - and in 2021, Facebook decided to rename its entire company to […]
author: Sean Hollister

title: Apple launches iOS 26.4 with AI playlists, purchase sharing, and more
summary: iOS 26.4 is here, and it comes with a bunch of small but notable updates. That includes a new Playlist Playground launching in beta in Apple Music, which uses AI to generate a song playlist - complete with a title, description, and tracklist - based on a text prompt. Apple Music is also adding a […]
author: Emma Roth

title: OpenClaw 太难搞，我用 Ribbi 这只青蛙接管了整条创意流程
summary:
站在风口上，猪都能飞起来

这是雷军在移动互联网刚兴起时的一句著名论断。

现在正值 AI 刚兴起的节点，我发现龙虾（OpenClaw）比猪还多。

title: 体验碎周报第 273 期（2026.3.23）

title: 设计素材周刊 206 期
summary: 来自「Moonvy 月维」的《设计素材周刊》，是一个长期更新的设计资源精选栏目。每周持续整理值得关注的设计素材、设计工具更新动态与优质资源，帮助设计师建立高效、稳定、可信赖的资源获取渠道。

title: 谷歌Stitch新功能实测，AI设计UI的5个趋势提前看
summary:
谷歌的王炸是接着一个又一个，Nano Banana 2发布还没一个月呢，旗下的 AI UI设计工具 Stitch 又更新了！

我实际体验下来，发现确实有不少新功能很有前瞻性，很可能会形成 AI 设计界面的新趋势和规范。但我也诚恳地说，它或许能在设计师的工作中提供一些帮助，但无法完全替代设计师。

title: AI产品叫好不叫座？5个策略9个案例告诉你如何让AI真正落地
summary:
最近 OpenClaw 龙虾有多火就不用我多说了，但是你发现了一个奇怪的现象没？

大多数热点都是在讲OpenClaw如何安装部署，却很少有人分享使用OpenClaw后，赚了钱或者给生活工作带来了什么样的便利。

你今天再上网，搜索热点就已经从 「OpenClaw 安装」变成 「OpenClaw卸载」。

title: 为什么选择 Moonvy 月维?
summary:
为什么不用 Figma 自身来交付

你可能有疑问，Figma 本身也可以交给工程师进行协作开发，为什么还需要一个第三方工具，这里就揭示给你为什么那么多团队使用 Moonvy 月维进行交付的理由：

更好用

Moonvy

title: 月维新增设计图字体下载功能
summary:
现在月维上的设计标注，可以支持下载字体文件了。

支持中文大部分免费商用字体，以及 Figma 中的设计师可以直接使用的用谷歌字体，都可以支持。设计师不用任何操作。

如何操作

  1. 打开 Moonvy 月维上的设计标注，选中文本字体，在右侧面板中找到文本属性

title: Figma 年度报告
summary:
2025 即将结束，回头看这一年在 Figma 里创建过的文件、版本，输出的设计图、切图、反复修改的页面

它们不是冷冰冰的数据，而是日复一日的坚持，是每一次交付前的专注与付出

我们团队已经 连续 3 年，都会为 Figma 做一个 年度数据总结的小工具，把 2025 年的努力「具像化」的呈现

通过 Figma 使用报告你可以清楚地看到：

  • 每一天的工作强度
  • 哪些周末在加班
  • 创建了多少文件
  • 修改了多少次设计
  • 一整年在 Fi
